description_long: This object is a vintage Sony cassette tape recorder, featuring a rectangular black body with a transparent hinged lid that opens to reveal the tape compartment. The front panel is equipped with several tactile control buttons, two large analog VU meters, and a series of labeled input/output ports. The left side displays XLR microphone inputs and line-out jacks, while the right side is mostly solid with minimal features. The top surface is partially perforated for ventilation, and the brand name 'SONY' is visible in white on the lid and side. The overall design is robust, with a mix of plastic and metal materials, and the device is intended for tabletop use in audio recording or playback scenarios. The object exudes a professional, utilitarian aesthetic typical of high-quality audio equipment from the late 20th century.
description: A black, rectangular Sony cassette tape recorder with a transparent lid, front control buttons, analog meters, and multiple input/output ports.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open transparent lid, tape compartment, control buttons, analog meters, and brand markings.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the XLR microphone inputs, line-out jacks, and the partially open lid.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid back panel, the open transparent lid, and minimal visible features.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the side profile, the open lid, and the SONY logo on the side.
